svg.text: • Build systems (Bazel, Make, Gradle): topological sort dependencies via DFS post-order.
• Module loaders / linkers: detect circular imports via gray-color cycle detection.
• Compilers: control-flow graph dominator analysis via DFS spanning tree.
• Filesystem tree walkers (find, du, npm install): natural recursion = DFS.
• Tarjan's SCC / Kosaraju in static analyzers (deadlock detection, data-flow).
• Garbage-collector mark phase (some GCs prefer DFS stack-locality vs BFS frontier).
• Game-tree search (chess, Go) — minimax, alpha-beta, MCTS rollouts.
DFS vs BFS — when to pick which
• structural questions (cycles, SCC, topological order, articulation points)
• deep narrow trees where memory must stay tight (only depth × call-frame space)
• backtracking search where you need to undo state on return
• shortest path in unweighted graphs, level-order, or bounded-depth questions
• graphs where DFS depth would overflow stack but BFS frontier fits
Both are O(V + E); pick by what the answer's structure resembles.
